name: Vintage Year
rationale: Older credits may not reflect current project performance, and their continued availability on the market may indicate weak demand -- a negative quality signal.
v0_6_change: Removed the pre-Paris override (cap at 20 for vintage < 2016). The 12-point-per-year decay already drives pre-2016 vintages to 0 (vintage 2017 = 8 points in 2026; vintage 2016 = 20 in 2026 which was the old cap anyway). The override was a discontinuity that caused inter-rater disagreement on the exact cutoff year (IRR study vintage κ=0.324, partly from this). For credits from 2015 or earlier, the formula alone produces scores ≤ 0, so the override was redundant.
notes: Vintage is the only dimension computable entirely on-chain from token metadata.
The 12 points per year decay rate is a provisional choice; expert calibration should validate.
v0.6: pre-Paris override removed. The formula floor at 0 handles all pre-2018 credits identically (score 0). No assessor judgment required for this dimension.
